title: myosotis scorpioides subsp. multiflora
intro: Myosotis scorpioides subsp. multiflora, also known as the multiflorous forget-me-not, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the borage family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The multiflorous forget-me-not has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it beneficial for relieving symptoms of fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and antiviral properties, contributing to its health benefits, including immune system support and protection against oxidative stress. Myosotis scorpioides subsp. multiflora can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and has been used in traditional folk medicine for centuries.
